2604.09175.title: Generalization and Scaling Laws for Mixture-of-Experts Transformers
2604.09175.paper_summary: We develop a theory of generalization and scaling for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Transformers that cleanly separates \emph{active} per-input capacity from routing combinatorics. By conditioning on fixed routing patterns and union-bounding across them, we derive a sup-norm covering-number bound whose metric entropy scales with the active parameter budget and incurs a MoE-specific routing overhead. Combined with a standard ERM analysis for squared loss, this yields a generalization bound under a $d$-dimensional manifold data model and $C^β$ targets, showing that approximation and estimation trade off as in dense networks once active parameters are accounted for appropriately. We further prove a constructive approximation theorem for MoE architectures, showing that, under the approximation construction, error can decrease either by scaling active capacity or by increasing the number of experts, depending on the dominant bottleneck. From these results we derive neural scaling laws for model size, data size, and compute-optimal tradeoffs. Overall, our results provide a transparent statistical reference point for reasoning about MoE scaling, clarifying which behaviors are certified by worst-case theory and which must arise from data-dependent routing structure or optimization dynamics.
2604.09175.paper_summary_zh: 我們為混合專家（MoE）Transformer 開發了一個泛化和擴展的理論，清晰地將每個輸入的\emph{活動}容量與路由組合學分開。通過對固定路由模式進行條件化並在它們之間進行聯合界限，我們推導出一個超範數覆蓋數界限，其度量熵隨著活動參數預算的增加而擴展，並產生特定於 MoE 的路由開銷。結合對平方損失的標準 ERM 分析，這在 $d$ 維流形數據模型和 $C^β$ 目標下產生了一個泛化界限，顯示出在適當考慮活動參數後，近似和估計之間的權衡就像在密集網絡中一樣。我們進一步證明了 MoE 架構的建設性近似定理，顯示在近似構造下，錯誤可以通過擴大活動容量或增加專家的數量來減少，具體取決於主導瓶頸。從這些結果中，我們推導出模型大小、數據大小和計算最佳權衡的神經擴展法則。總的來說，我們的結果為思考 MoE 擴展提供了一個透明的統計參考點，澄清了哪些行為是由最壞情況理論所認證的，哪些必須源於數據依賴的路由結構或優化動態。
2604.09175.author: Mansour Zoubeirou a Mayaki et.al.
